script:
Hello, this is The Portfolio Podcast for MEME. Thursday — here is your MEME recap. The portfolio closed up zero point four three percent on the session. Nine primary positions. Mixed tape. Let us get into it.

Nebius Group is validating its first Vera Rubin NVL seventy-two rack in Finland — days after Nvidia disclosed a nine point three percent ownership stake in the company. That is not a financial investment. That is a strategic signal.

No broad macro data was available for Thursday's session. Markets appear to have taken directional cues from Alphabet's raised artificial intelligence capital expenditure forecast, which rippled across the data center and infrastructure supply chain. The Nasdaq composite absorbed that catalyst and responded accordingly, though no index-level closing figure was available at time of production.

The top story belongs to Nebius Group. Nvidia's nine point three percent disclosed stake was already significant on its own — a hyperscaler validator choosing to own nearly a tenth of an artificial intelligence infrastructure company is not a routine filing. But Thursday brought an operational counterpart: Nebius confirmed it has validated its first Vera Rubin NVL seventy-two rack in a Finnish data center, making it one of the earliest showcase deployments of Nvidia's most advanced infrastructure platform. According to reporting from finance dot yahoo dot com, Nebius gains early hardware access through this relationship while Nvidia gains a high-profile proof point for its next-generation systems. The year-to-date share price return sits at one hundred forty-two point five three percent, though the thirty-day return is down twenty-three point zero eight percent. The stock remains under scrutiny for fair value — but Thursday's hardware milestone added a layer of fundamental credibility to what had looked, to some, like a momentum story.

Applied Optoelectronics was up one point three six percent on Thursday's session. The broader photonic and optical networking group moved higher, with reporting noting that investor interest in photonic stocks surged as hyperscalers ramp artificial intelligence infrastructure spending — Alphabet's capital expenditure announcement was cited as a primary catalyst. Watch for continued hyperscaler commentary in the earnings cycle for confirmation of transceiver demand.

Redwire Corporation was up three point two three percent on Thursday's session — the strongest single-session gain among primary holdings. A published analysis noted the stock is down forty-two percent over the last year and trading under ten dollars, raising the question of whether the dip constitutes opportunity or a value trap. The piece stopped short of a clear answer. Watch for any contract announcements or government space program news that could provide a near-term narrative anchor.

AST SpaceMobile was down four point four seven percent on Thursday's session, making it the session's largest decliner in the portfolio. Clear Street maintained a constructive view, characterizing the stock's roughly fifty percent pullback as a buying opportunity ahead of second-quarter earnings, attributing weakness to third-party launch vehicle disruptions rather than lost contracts or demand deterioration. Separately, AT&T reported strong wireless subscriber growth in the second quarter, which RBC described as keeping its investment thesis intact — relevant context given AT&T's role as a distribution partner for direct-to-device satellite connectivity. Earnings will be the next hard test.

Nebius Group was up one point two nine percent on Thursday's session. The news flow was covered in the top story. Three separate developments — the Nvidia stake, the Vera Rubin rack validation, and a broader lift from Alphabet's capital expenditure guidance — converged on a single session. Thirty-day price action remains negative, which means the setup is volatile. Watch for any follow-on institutional disclosures.

Bloom Energy was down zero point four two percent on Thursday's session. RBC flagged that EdgeMode's newly announced projects in Panama could represent an expansion of its existing partnership with Bloom Energy — a potential signal of geographic revenue diversification. Additionally, Bloom Energy heads into its second-quarter earnings report with surging estimates and an Oracle fuel cell deal underpinning demand expectations, though analysts noted the valuation carries a premium. Earnings are the immediate catalyst.

TeraWulf was up two point eight seven percent on Thursday's session. No company-specific news was available. The move aligns directionally with strength across the bitcoin mining and artificial intelligence infrastructure peer group, both of which benefited from the day's technology tailwinds. Watch for any hash rate or energy infrastructure updates.

Lumentum Holdings was up zero point four seven percent on Thursday's session. A published report specifically named Lumentum among stocks positioned to benefit from big technology companies' artificial intelligence spending increases, citing Alphabet's capital expenditure guidance as a direct demand driver for data center optical components. The position remains modest in Thursday's move but the structural thesis got reinforcement.

IREN Limited was down one point seven zero percent on Thursday's session. A separate analysis raised the question of whether the stock is undervalued following its two point eight billion dollar artificial intelligence cloud contract win with clients including Microsoft and Nvidia, lifting its twenty twenty-six artificial intelligence cloud annualized recurring revenue target above four billion dollars. The thirty-day price decline of roughly twenty-seven percent is the tension point. Contract scale versus near-term price action — that is the story.

Sandisk Corporation was up zero point six nine percent on Thursday's session. A published piece characterized Sandisk as an emerging artificial intelligence memory standout with surging revenues and improving profitability, drawing a direct comparison to Nvidia's trajectory. Earnings visibility on memory demand will be the next data point to watch.

Three items for the next forty-eight hours: Bloom Energy's second-quarter earnings release — revenue guidance and fuel cell demand commentary will be closely watched. AST SpaceMobile's upcoming second-quarter earnings — launch cadence and subscriber projections are the key metrics. Any follow-on Alphabet capital expenditure disclosures or hyperscaler earnings commentary that could move the optical and artificial intelligence infrastructure supply chain.

This is The Portfolio Podcast for MEME. We will be back same time the next business day with tomorrow's news. Watch Bloom Energy's earnings call closely — the Panama data center angle and Oracle fuel cell demand could set the tone for the entire clean energy infrastructure segment.
